GNN Drug Ranking Report

Dataset: GEO GSE166635  |  Generated: 2026-04-20 09:59  |  Notebook: 03_gnn_drug_ranking.ipynb
What this report covers: A Graph Neural Network (GNN) pipeline that learns drug–gene interaction scores from the prioritised hub gene network built in notebook 02. Three GNN architectures are trained and compared (GCN, GAT, GraphSAGE) on a bipartite graph of 547 target genes and 4,978 drug candidates connected by 8,027 known interactions. The best-performing model re-scores every drug–gene pair and produces a final ranked list of repurposing candidates. The GNN score refines the composite score from P3 by incorporating structural information from the interaction graph — drugs that are well-connected to highly-scored hub genes receive a higher ranking even if their direct interaction evidence is sparse.

Training loss curves

Training and validation MSE loss per epoch for all three models. Highlighted panel = best model.

Metric comparison

R², MSE, and MAE grouped bar chart across all models on the held-out test set.

Predicted vs. actual scores

Scatter plot of GNN-predicted vs. true composite scores on the test set. Dashed line = perfect prediction.

4 · Drug Ranking

Drugs are ranked by their GNN-predicted interaction score with the target genes. A higher score indicates stronger predicted therapeutic relevance based on the graph structure. The score delta (GNN score − original composite score) highlights drugs the GNN promotes or demotes relative to the rule-based ranking.

20
approved drugs in top 20
4978
unique drugs ranked
547
target genes covered

Top 25 drug candidates ranked by GraphSAGE GNN score. ★ = approved. | = original composite score. Colour = clinical phase.

Drug–gene interaction network

Bipartite network of top 20 GNN-predicted drug–gene pairs. Gene size = number of top drug connections. Drug colour = clinical phase. Gold border = FDA approved. Edge width & opacity = GNN score.
